name: implement-init-redesign
description: Implement the /tf init redesign per docs/init-redesign-plan.md (Round 1 patched). Creates extensions/init.ts, modifies extensions/index.ts, creates test/init.test.ts, updates package.json + README.md.

phases.task:
Implement the design plan in `/Users/beta/work/personal-projects/pi-taskflow/docs/init-redesign-plan.md`. This is a redesign of the `/tf init` command for the pi-taskflow project (a workflow orchestration extension for the Pi coding agent).

# Project context

- Working directory: `/Users/beta/work/personal-projects/pi-taskflow`
- TypeScript with strict mode, ESM, `verbatimModuleSyntax: true` (use `import type` for type-only imports)
- Test runner: `node --experimental-strip-types --test` (see `package.json` `scripts.test`)
- Typecheck: `npm run typecheck` (runs `tsc --noEmit`)
- Pi extension UI primitives in `node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-coding-agent/dist/core/extensions/types.d.ts` — see `ExtensionUIContext` interface around line 67. Methods: `select`, `input`, `confirm`, `notify`, `custom`. `Model<Api>` type in `node_modules/@earendil-works/pi-ai/dist/types.d.ts` (has `id`, `name`, `provider`, `reasoning`, `input: ('text'|'image')[]`, `cost`, `contextWindow`).

# Files to create/modify (in this order)

## 1. CREATE `extensions/init.ts` (new, ~250 lines)

The single source of truth for init logic. Read §3.1 of the design plan in full and implement every export listed. Key constraints:

- **Use the existing `writeFileAtomic` from `./store.ts:760`** for `writeSettings()`. Do NOT hand-roll atomic write.
- **`InitRole` has a `defaultModel: string` field** — `RECOMMENDED_DEFAULTS` is derived from `INIT_ROLES`, not stored separately.
- **`InitFlowResult` is a discriminated union** (3 variants: `saved`, `no-change`, `cancelled`). `cancelled` MUST NOT carry `chosen`.
- **`buildRoleOptions(...)` takes a single context object** `{ current?, recommended? }`, not separate args.
- **`buildRoleOptions(...)` must omit the "Keep current" footer entry** when `ctx.current === undefined` (first-run).
- **`diffRoles(...)` returns 4 statuses**: `unchanged`, `changed`, `new`, `stale-preserved`. `stale-preserved` is for keys in `before` that are NOT in the catalog (preserved in the merge but flagged).
- **`readSettings()` validates `modelRoles`**: missing file → `{}`; malformed JSON → throw; `modelRoles` not a plain object → coerce to `{}` with a one-time `console.warn`.
- **`parseCustomModel(input)`** handles 2+ segment slashes (e.g. `vercel-ai-gateway/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-6`): split on first `/`, treat everything after as `id`. Reject empty provider, empty id, or no slash.
- **`runInteractiveInit(...)`** is the full UX flow. It must handle:
  - First-run short-circuit: empty `currentRoles` → 2-option gate ("Apply recommended defaults" / "Configure manually")
  - Action menu: 5 options when `currentRoles` non-empty
  - Per-role picker (loop or "Edit one role" jump)
  - Preview screen (diffRoles) with [Save, Edit a role, Cancel]
  - Esc semantics: action menu `undefined` → cancelled; picker `undefined` → back to action menu (partial picks kept); custom input `undefined` → fall back to `current ?? recommended`
  - Short-circuit `{kind: "no-change"}` when picks are identical to current
  - Custom-input models not in registry → save anyway + `ctx.ui.notify("warning", ...)`
- **Role catalog** (INIT_ROLES) — copy the 6 roles from the current code at `extensions/index.ts:282-289` (the `roleDescs` record) and `extensions/index.ts:578-587` (the `roleDefs` array). The descriptions are slightly different between the two — prefer the longer `roleDefs` descriptions. Recommended defaults (for the `defaultModel` field):
  - fast: `openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash`
  - strong: `openrouter/xiaomi/mimo-v2.5-pro`
  - thinker: `openrouter/deepseek/deepseek-v4-pro`
  - arbiter: `openrouter/qwen/qwen3.7-max`
  - vision: `minimax/MiniMax-M3`
  - reasoner: `z-ai/glm-5.1`
- **Role-aware filtering/sorting**: `vision` should only include models with `input.includes("image")`; `thinker`/`arbiter`/`reasoner` should sort `reasoning: true` models first (but don't filter, since some users may want a non-reasoning fast model for thinker).

## 2. MODIFY `extensions/index.ts`

Three changes (use the current file as ground truth — re-read it before editing):

### 2.a Tool path (currently lines 277-320)

Replace the entire `if (action === "init")` block with the new logic per §3.2.a + §3.2.a.1 of the design plan. Specifically:
- Read `params.mode` (default: `"show"`)
- `mode === "show"` → call `formatRolesReport(current)` and return text
- `mode === "apply-defaults"` → require `params.force === true`, then merge `RECOMMENDED_DEFAULTS` with stale keys preserved (per §3.2.a code), then `writeSettings()`, return diff report
- `mode === "interactive"` → require `ctx.hasUI`, then call `runInteractiveInit(...)`, return formatted result
- Unknown mode → `errorResult(action, ...)`
- **v0.0.13 deprecation bridge** (per §3.2.a.1): if `params.mode` is OMITTED and `settings.modelRoles` is empty/missing, fall back to v0.0.12 behavior (write `RECOMMENDED_DEFAULTS` non-interactively) + emit `console.warn` deprecation notice. If `params.mode` is OMITTED and `modelRoles` exists, behave as `mode="show"`. (This branch is for `action === "init"` with no `mode` field; `mode` field present goes to the explicit branches above.)

### 2.b Tool schema (currently `TaskflowParams` around line 53-95)

Add two new fields to the typebox schema per §3.4.1:
- `mode: Type.Optional(StringEnum(["show", "apply-defaults", "interactive"] as const, { description: "...", default: "show" }))`
- `force: Type.Optional(Type.Boolean({ description: "..." }))`

`StringEnum` is already imported from `@earendil-works/pi-ai` at line 16. Do NOT add a new import.

### 2.c Slash path (currently lines 570-650)

Replace the `if (sub === "init")` block with a thin wrapper around `runInteractiveInit(...)` from the new module. The non-UI fallback (for `ctx.hasUI === false`) should:
- If `currentRoles` non-empty: `ctx.ui.notify("info", ...)` showing current roles
- If empty: `ctx.ui.notify("warning", "No modelRoles configured. Run /tf init in an interactive session to select models.")`

The deprecation bridge in §2.a only applies to the tool path, NOT the slash path.

## 3. CREATE `test/init.test.ts`

Cover the test plan in §6 of the design plan. All tests use `node:test` (no jest/vitest). Use the helper pattern from `test/agents.test.ts:18-32` for tmpdir + agent-dir override (use `PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR` env var, see `test/agents.test.ts:8`).

The mock UI context for `runInteractiveInit` tests should:
- Record every `select`/`input` call
- Return canned answers from a queue
- Implement just `select`, `input`, `notify` (the 3 methods init uses)

Required test cases (from §6):
- `formatModelOption`: includes name + provider/id, reasoning tag, image tag, omits tag for text-only
- `buildRoleOptions`: current marker, recommended marker, separator, Custom + Keep current entries, vision role filters text-only, thinker role sorts reasoning-first
- `parseCustomModel`: parses "openrouter/x/y", parses "vercel-ai-gateway/a/b" (3+ segments), rejects "no-slash", rejects "", rejects "provider/"
- `diffRoles`: unchanged / changed / new / stale-preserved; diff order matches catalog
- `readSettings` / `writeSettings`: round-trip preserves non-modelRoles, write is atomic (verify unique-tmp naming + cleanup-on-error), missing file → {}, malformed JSON throws, `modelRoles: []` or `""` or `"string"` → {}
- `INIT_ROLES`: every role has non-empty description AND non-empty `defaultModel`
- `RECOMMENDED_DEFAULTS`: derived from `INIT_ROLES`
- `buildRoleOptions` (empty state): `ctx.current` undefined → no "Keep current"; set → "Keep current" present
- `runInteractiveInit` (mocked ui):
  - empty `currentRoles` → 2-option action menu
  - "Apply recommended defaults" → `{kind: "saved", chosen: RECOMMENDED_DEFAULTS, savedPath}`
  - "Configure each role" with "Keep current" picks → `{kind: "saved", chosen: current}`
  - all picks identical to current → `{kind: "no-change", chosen: current}`, no dialog
  - "Cancel" on action menu → `{kind: "cancelled"}`
  - Esc on action menu (undefined return) → `{kind: "cancelled"}` (not confused with custom input Esc)
  - Custom model not in registry → still saves, but warns

## 4. MODIFY `package.json`

Add `test/init.test.ts` to the `scripts.test` command. The current `test` script is:
```
"test": "PI_TASKFLOW_BUILTIN_AGENTS_DIR= node --experimental-strip-types --test test/interpolate.test.ts test/condition.test.ts test/schema.test.ts test/usage.test.ts test/runtime.test.ts test/features.test.ts test/runner.test.ts test/store.test.ts test/agents.test.ts test/render.test.ts test/desugar.test.ts test/cache.test.ts test/loop.test.ts test/tournament.test.ts"
```

Insert `test/init.test.ts` somewhere alphabetically reasonable (after `test/agents.test.ts`).

## 5. MODIFY `README.md`

Update the `/tf init` documentation section (currently around line 372 and 450-498). The new example should reflect:
- Action menu at the top
- Picker shows model display name + provider/id + capability flags
- Preview screen before save
- New tool path semantics: `mode: "show" | "apply-defaults" | "interactive"`, `force: true` for `apply-defaults`
- Note the v0.0.13 deprecation bridge for the tool path

# Constraints

- **No new dependencies** — use what's already in `package.json`.
- **`verbatimModuleSyntax: true`** — use `import type` for type-only imports.
- **No changes to test/e2e.mts** unless you find a real e2e gap while implementing. If you do, document it in the implementation report but don't change the file.
- **Run `npm run typecheck` and `npm test` at the end** and fix any issues. Report test counts in the final summary.
- **If a `writeFileAtomic` rename fails with EPERM/EACCES on your platform**, log a `console.warn` and re-throw — the tool path will catch it. Don't try to fall back to non-atomic write inside `writeFileAtomic` itself; that's R10's mitigation, and the slash path can choose how to surface it.
- **Do not modify `extensions/store.ts`** — `writeFileAtomic` is exported (line 760) and you just import it.

# Output

When done, report:
1. Files created / modified, with line counts
2. Test count: how many tests, how many pass
3. Typecheck: pass/fail
4. Any deviations from the plan and why
5. Any reviewer-flagged issues you found while implementing (use them in your own self-review)
6. Anything you deferred or punted to a follow-up
